item.title: Hugging Face confirms breach affected internal datasets and credentials, urges users to take action
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Hugging Face is urging users to rotate any access tokens stored on the platform and review account activity.

item.title: StrictlyVC returns to New York City September 10 to celebrate a huge year for the city’s startup community
item.dc:creator: Morgan Little
item.description: For the first time since 2024, StrictlyVC is coming back to New York City — and we're bringing the kind of access you’d expect from an under-wraps event to the whole startup, VC, and dealmaking community.

item.title: Netflix paid $587M for Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking startup
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Netflix revealed that it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck.

item.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

item.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."

item.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Current AI, a nonprofit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat, and more.

item.title: Waymo says San Francisco service has resumed after one-hour pause
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: This isn’t the first time power outages have caused issues for Waymo.

item.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

item.title: All the EVs that were discontinued or killed off in the U.S. this year
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Th Honda Prologue will no longer be sold in the U.S., joining a growing list of EV models to exit the market this year.

item.title: Federal employees can download TikTok on their work phones again
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The Department of Justice says that federal employees can now download TikTok on their government devices.

item.title: A 600-mile road trip (and data) proves EV charging doesn’t suck anymore
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: A recent road trip in an EV revealed just how much faster and more reliable DC Fast charging has become in the U.S.

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Applications close in 48 hours — here’s everything Australian founders need to know about Stripe x Startup Battlefield
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: The window is almost shut. On August 19, eight startups will take the stage at Stripe Tour Sydney in front of investors, global press, and the Australian tech community. One startup walks away with automatic entry into TechCrunch Disrupt in San Francisco — no application, no further competition, a guaranteed spot on the world’s most […]

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.
